entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
This course includeds the following topic(s): Grammar/Comp: Grade 9, Dictation: Grade 9, Composition: Grade 9, Commonplace: Grades 9-12

About Grammar/Comp: Grade 9
This course works alongside Michael Clay Thompson's Essay Voyage lessons. It invites students to explore writing concepts such as tone, style, point of view, and more. Students also experiment with multiple written forms and gain familiarity with key habits of academic writing.

About Dictation: Grade 9
Students use passages from their books to practice dictation skills.

About Commonplace: Grades 9-12
Building on the habit of copywork in younger grades, high school students copy passages of any length that strike them from their readings into their commonplace book. For some students this becomes a lifelong keepsake and continuing habit.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content:
Grammar/Comp: Grade 9
New students entering Grade 9 without much former Grammar instruction should complete MCT's Grammar and Practice Voyage scheduled in Grade 8. Composition prompts often reference Alveary lessons in Literature, History, Geography, Bible, etc., so it is ideal if students are using Alveary subjects across the curriculum. However, lessons include the lesson topic and target skill/form, along with ideas for substitution when possible to make the lessons flexible for teachers and students.

Dictation: Grade 9
Depending on the student, dictation may or may not be necessary at this level.

Commonplace: Grades 9-12
Students new to copywork or keeping a commonplace book may want to schedule this during morning lessons to build the habit.
